text:
Canada’s Alysha Newman has called for a designated ‘coaching section’ for track and field events at theOlympic Gamesafter her coach ‘almost got kicked out of his seat’ during the women’s pole vault final.

Newman, 30, won Canada’s first ever Olympic medal in the women’s pole vault on Wednesday after clearing a national-record height of 4.85m, securing her bronze at a packed-out Stade de France.

Nina Kennedy soared over a height of 4.90m to get her hands on gold forAustralia, while Katie Moon, representing the United States, went home with the silver medal inParis.

The women’s competition came after Armand Duplantis’ historic performance in the men’s pole vault final, which saw the Swede break the world record for a ninth time.

Given it had been five years since Newman’s most recent participation in a major final, the Canadian was delighted with her showing on the biggest stage and was clearly enjoying her moment in the limelight.

After successfully making it over the 4.85m mark with her second attempt, Newman initially appeared to have picked up an injury before breaking into a magnificent twerking celebration – prompting loud cheers from the nearby fans.

The celebration has since gone viral across social media, with Newman – who also models on OnlyFans – later joking that she may have to get a photograph of the moment framed.

Tam 112 yÄ±l sonra bir KanadalÄ± sÄ±rÄ±kla atlamada madalya kazandÄ± ve 4.85’le Kanada rekoru kÄ±rdÄ±ÄÄ± atlayÄ±Åtan sonra twerk yaptÄ±!KarÅÄ±nÄ±zda, the one and only Alysha Newman!pic.twitter.com/nwwwtPZdMG

Speaking at a press conference immediately after the action, an ecstatic Newman said she ‘loved every moment’ of the final in the French capital.

However, the bronze-medallist admitted she was close to ‘sharing some words’ with stewards inside the stadium after her coach almost got himself booted out of his seat in the stands.

‘It’s been five years since I’ve been in a final, so for me to wrap around and jump pretty intense, back-to-back with 48 hours in between, was a challenge for me to not get to excited, but not be too relaxed,’ Newman told reporters.

‘But I would say that the one thing that really kind of got me angry… not mad or angry… but fuelled me actually more was looking over in the stands and seeing my coach almost getting kicked out of his seat.

‘So I definitely had to take a couple of breaths for that because my coach is my best friend. He would do anything for me and I would do the same.

‘I was very close to sharing some words over there and really letting them know that of course we understand that there’s more than 12 girls in the final, but it’s important and it’s an event that needs coaches present.

‘Maybe future-wise, we look at getting coaches between the stands and where the photographers are and maybe a little coaching section.

‘And you guys could sell more tickets and make more money and then we could have those coaches down on the in-field that we can actually communicate with and not feel like we’re stopping people’s view as well. That was hard for me.’

Newman claimed she had to have ‘tunnel vision’ focus in order to keep Moon and Kennedy in her sights during the final.

‘I loved every moment of it and I just really tried to stay tunnel vision with these girls,’ she continued.

‘I knew I had to jump something high because Katie and Nina have been at these heights for a while.

‘These heights are new for me but they’ve been something that I’ve been thinking about for a while now.’

title: Pole vaulter Alysha Newman calls for major change at Olympics after her twerking celebration goes viral | Metro News
description: Alysha Newman has demanded a coaching section for track and field events at the Olympics after her coach almost got kicked out of his seat.
